title: Terms implied into a consumer contract by the Consumer Rights Act 2015
summary: 9084 Contract — CRA 2015 quality, fitness, description, and digital content standards.
learningObjectives: Outline implied terms under CRA 2015 for goods and services.
Explain satisfactory quality, fitness for purpose, as described.
Analyse consumer remedies: repair, replacement, refund.
Compare CRA with Sale of Goods Act for business contracts.

simpleExplanation.title: Consumer protection by default
simpleExplanation.summary: The Consumer Rights Act 2015 automatically implies quality standards into B2C contracts — goods must match description and be fit for purpose.
steps: Goods — satisfactory quality, fit for purpose.
Digital content — same standards as goods.
Remedies — tiered: repair/replace then price reduction/refund.

flashcards.back: Business-to-**consumer** contracts for goods, services, and digital content — replaces much of SGA 1979 for consumers.
Goods must meet standard a reasonable person would regard as satisfactory — considering description, price, and other circumstances.
Goods must be fit for purpose consumer makes known to trader — express or implied.
Service performed with **reasonable care and skill**; if price/time not agreed, reasonable price within reasonable time.
Consumer may reject within **30 days** if goods do not conform — full refund (s20–22).
After 30 days or if consumer chooses — trader must repair or replace; if impossible, price reduction or final right to reject.

sections.content: **CRA 2015 implied terms** (3.2.2) — know **statutory standards** and **consumer remedies**.

sections.question: Maya buys a laptop online from TechCo for £600, describing it as 'brand new, 8GB RAM'. On delivery the laptop has 4GB RAM and scratches. Maya rejects it within 10 days. Advise Maya's remedies under CRA 2015.
sections.solution:
**Consumer contract:** Maya is a **consumer**; TechCo a **trader** — **CRA 2015** applies.

**Implied terms breached:**
- **As described** (s11) — 4GB not 8GB.
- **Satisfactory quality** (s9) — scratches on 'new' laptop.
- **Fit for purpose** (s10) — if Maya specified use requiring 8GB.

**Non-conforming goods:** Goods **do not conform** to contract (s19).

**Remedy — short-term right to reject (s20–22):** Within **30 days**, Maya may **reject** and demand **full refund** — no obligation to accept repair first.

**Trader's duty:** TechCo must **refund without undue delay** and within 14 days of receiving goods back.

**Conclusion:** Maya entitled to **reject and full refund** — express description and implied quality terms both breached.
